behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly output-quality validation (grammar + meaning preservation), which depends on real model judgments and variability; stubs would hide real failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior describes a single model output check, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a curated bilingual evaluation set through the real NMT API, score adequacy/fluency (or equivalent semantic-quality rubric), and fail when meaning preservation or grammatical coherence drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff: API client error handling -> fallback translator -> response shaping. A deterministic 503 stub is sufficient to reproduce this path. Rejected System: behavior text only requires handling a 503 response, not real network timeout/socket behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: correctness does not depend on model output quality or multi-step model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client adapter (stubbed to return 503) + SQLite-backed fallback module; assert caller gets successful fallback translation and no propagated error.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: This behavior crosses module/query boundary with a real local SQLite store; repository SQL/schema mismatches are real failures not caught by pure unit mocks. Rejected System: local DB wiring is explicitly in-process/local infrastructure under the Integration definition. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a test SQLite DB with known phrase pairs, call fallback translator through service/repository boundary, and assert exact stored translation is returned for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Behavior combines computation plus persistence. Real failure can occur in handoff/mapping when saving computed metric, not only in the distance algorithm itself. Rejected Agentic: strings can be deterministic fixtures; no quality judgment of model output required. Rejected System: no required real network/subprocess/OS primitive beyond local module+store wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke comparison pipeline with fixed primary/fallback strings, assert computed character-level edit distance matches expected value, and verify persisted metric record fields are correct.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state-handoff across local modules (primary result selection + async/background metric logging). Can be caught with integrated in-process components and controlled async execution. Rejected Agentic: behavior text checks routing/preference/logging, not whether model translation quality is good. Rejected Workflow: no multiple model decisions in sequence are required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with deterministic primary and fallback providers both succeeding; assert response uses primary output immediately, then assert background job/event persisted quality comparison record.
